name: Grok TTS
description: xAI's Grok text-to-speech model. Generates high-fidelity spoken audio in 5 expressive voices (eve, ara, rex, sal, leo) with 20+ supported languages. Supports inline speech tags for laughter, whispers, and pauses.

examples.name: Simple Generation
examples.description: Generate speech with the default voice (eve) in English

input.text: Hello! Welcome to the xAI Text to Speech API.

code_snippets.label: typescript
curl

input.text: Thank you for calling. How can I help you today?

code_snippets.label: typescript
curl

examples.name: High-Fidelity MP3
examples.description: 44.1 kHz / 192 kbps MP3 for production use

input.text: Crystal clear audio at maximum quality.

code_snippets.label: typescript
curl

examples.name: Telephony (mulaw)
examples.description: G.711 μ-law at 8 kHz for SIP / PSTN integration

input.text: Hello, thank you for calling. How can I help you today?

code_snippets.label: typescript
curl

examples.name: Expressive Delivery
examples.description: Inline speech tags for laughter, pauses, and whispers

input.text: So I walked in and [pause] there it was. [laugh] I honestly could not believe it! <whisper>It was a secret the whole time.</whisper>

code_snippets.label: typescript
curl

examples.name: Text Normalization
examples.description: Convert written numbers and abbreviations to spoken form

input.text: The total is $1,234.56 and the meeting is at 3pm on Jan 15th.

code_snippets.label: typescript
curl

metadata.Voices: eve, ara, rex, sal, leo (+ custom)
metadata.Languages: 20+ BCP-47 codes (en, fr, de, ja, zh, ...) plus auto-detect

language.description: BCP-47 language code (e.g. "en", "zh", "pt-BR") or "auto" for automatic language detection. Required — xAI returns 400 if omitted. Supported codes: auto, en, ar-EG, ar-SA, ar-AE, bn, zh, fr, de, hi, id, it, ja, ko, pt-BR, pt-PT, ru, es-MX, es-ES, tr, vi.

optimize_streaming_latency.description: Latency optimization for streaming synthesis. 0 (default): no optimization, best audio quality. 1: reduced first-chunk size for lower time-to-first-audio with minor quality tradeoff.

output_format.description: Output audio format. Defaults to MP3 at 24 kHz / 128 kbps when omitted.

bit_rate.description: Bit rate in bps. MP3 only. Defaults to 128000. Supported: 32000, 64000, 96000, 128000, 192000.

codec.description: Audio codec. Defaults to "mp3". mp3 → audio/mpeg (general use); wav → audio/wav (lossless); pcm → audio/pcm (raw 16-bit LE, real-time pipelines); mulaw/ulaw → audio/basic (G.711 μ-law, telephony); alaw → audio/alaw (G.711 A-law, telephony).

sample_rate.description: Sample rate in Hz. Defaults to 24000. Supported: 8000, 16000, 22050, 24000, 44100, 48000. Telephony codecs (mulaw, alaw) typically use 8000.

text.description: Text to convert to speech. Maximum 15,000 characters. Supports inline speech tags: [pause], [laugh], <whisper>…</whisper>, etc.

text_normalization.description: When true, normalizes written-form text into spoken-form before synthesis (e.g. "Dr." → "Doctor", "100" → "one hundred"). Defaults to false.

voice_id.description: Voice for synthesis. Defaults to "eve". Built-in voices: eve (energetic), ara (warm), rex (confident), sal (balanced), leo (authoritative). Custom voice IDs from /v1/tts/voices are also accepted. Case-insensitive — "Eve", "EVE", and "eve" are equivalent.

audio.description: Presigned R2 URL for the generated audio file. MIME type reflects the requested codec (audio/mpeg for mp3, audio/wav for wav, etc.).
